description_long: This is a vintage-style television with a woodgrain-patterned rectangular casing and a convex glass screen. The front features a large, dark display area bordered by a thick, beige bezel, with two rotary dials and a speaker grille on the right side. The top of the unit is flat and finished in faux wood, with a metal loop antenna extending upward from the rear. The back is black plastic with ventilation grilles and a label indicating the brand 'CrownVision' along with various technical details. The sides are smooth and unadorned, continuing the woodgrain finish. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century CRT televisions.
description: A vintage boxy television with a woodgrain finish, convex glass screen, rotary dials, and a metal loop antenna.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, beige bezel, two rotary dials, speaker grille, and control buttons.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the woodgrain finish and the depth of the television's casing.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the black plastic back, ventilation grilles, brand label, and the base of the metal antenna.
description_view_3: Right side view with a clear look at the woodgrain finish and the television's overall boxy profile.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick beige plastic bezel with a matte finish and rounded corners, integrated with rotary dials and speaker grille.
attribute_control_button_layout: Clustered on the bottom right of the front panel, including rotary dials and push buttons.
